text:
User: instead of a notes button, let me swipe right on the screen to go to a notes screen -- just like on tiktok you can swipe right to go to someone's profile -- same idea.

But the notes shouldn't just be a notes, it should be a note but it should run the agent, like maybe I write "this felt kinda weird, it hurt my back a little" and maybe the agent writes back "ok, try with 50% less weight and tell me how it feels" and it would adjust the next set's weight, or maybe the agent decides "ok, let's skip this one for a few sessions and ease back into it in two weeks" and makes notes and whatnot to change the program, etc. [...] - **Swipe right** = the exercise's **Coach** screen (TikTok-style side panel).
- **Remove Log & Next** — advancing = logging, implicitly, on swipe. [...] ## 3. Swipe-right Coach screen
**SessionView.swift** — horizontal `TabView(.page(indexDisplayMode: .never))` with Coach at tag 0 (left) and Runner at tag 1 (right), defaulting to the runner; orthogonal to the runner's vertical paging ScrollView. **RunnerView.swift** reads steps from the session, binds `scrollPosition` to `$session.currentStepID`, and shows a faint leading "Coach ‹" edge hint. **CoachView.swift** — full-bleed dark, scoped to the current exercise, terse transcript (quote-glyph coach lines, trailing secondary user lines, green branch-glyph applied-diff lines), a seeded opener, a glass input bar, and the tappable "Deload 2 weeks" follow-up chip. The keyword policy (pain/tired/easy/great/default) appends replies and mutates upcoming sets in the shared session, so edits show on the runner's next pages and in the list sheet (which now renders skipped/edited sets). [...] - **Remove the Note button** — notes now live on the Coach screen. [...] - **Remove the Note button** — notes moved into the Coach screen. [...] Advancing is swipe-down only. **ControlsView.swift** dropped the Log & Next and Note buttons. The timer no longer emphasizes any button — **StepPageView.swift**'s `TimedHeroView` is now self-contained: at zero it fires the success haptic and shows a quiet "Swipe down for next ⌄" hint. A **Finish** affordance appears only on the last step (there's otherwise no terminal action).
